unit.name: Rectified flow training and sampling
unit.description: Gives the concrete rectified-flow training and sampling algorithms.
unit.summary: Rectified flow is presented as a concrete, simple diffusion implementation with a noise distribution pnoise and a data distribution pdata [ts=2216s]. Each training step samples z from the simple prior, x from the dataset, and t uniformly from [0,1] [ts=2232s], [ts=2251s]. It then defines a straight-line interpolation xt between x and z and a velocity vector v=z-x from the clean data point toward the noise sample [ts=2262s], [ts=2277s]. The neural network f_theta(xt,t) is trained to predict that velocity using mean squared error [ts=2306s], [ts=2358s]. The lecturer emphasizes that, unlike GANs, diffusion/rectified-flow training gives a meaningful loss curve that usually decreases as the model improves [ts=2367s]. For sampling, one chooses a fixed step count, often T=50, samples pure noise, then marches backward from t=1 to t=0 [ts=2431s], [ts=2445s]. At each step the model predicts vt and the sample is updated by stepping against the predicted noise direction, gradually removing noise until x0 is obtained [ts=2471s], [ts=2542s].

key_points.text: Rectified flow uses a simple pnoise and the target pdata.
Each training iteration samples z~pnoise, x~pdata, and t~Uniform(0,1).
Rectified flow sets xt as a linear interpolation between x and z and v=z-x.
The network f_theta(xt,t) is trained to predict the velocity vector v.
The training loss is mean squared error between the ground-truth velocity and model prediction.
Diffusion training regains a meaningful loss curve compared with GAN training.
Sampling usually chooses a fixed number of steps, with T=50 a common starting point.
During sampling, the model predicts vt at each noise level and updates the sample by stepping against that vector.

kp_catalog.name: Rectified flow formulation
kp_catalog.description: Rectified flow trains a network to predict a velocity vector along a straight path between data and noise.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because rectified flow formulation formalizes how rectified flow trains a network to predict a velocity vector along a straight path between data and noise, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Rectified flow sampling
kp_catalog.description: Sampling marches from t=1 noise to t=0 data by repeatedly applying predicted velocity updates.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because rectified flow sampling clarifies sampling marches from t=1 noise to t=0 data by repeatedly applying predicted velocity updates, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
